651	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) probeQUIC(addr string, tlsConfig *tls.Config, ch chan error) {
652		startTime := time.Now()
653

657		}
658		ctx, cancel := context.WithDeadline(context.Background(), time.Now().Add(t))
659		defer cancel()

169	// Exchange implements the [Upstream] interface for *dnsOverQUIC.
170	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) Exchange(req *dns.Msg) (resp *dns.Msg, err error) {
171		// When sending queries over a QUIC connection, the DNS Message ID MUST be
172		// set to 0.  The stream mapping for DoQ allows for unambiguous correlation
173		// of queries and responses, so the Message ID field is not required.
174		//
175		// See https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9250#section-4.2.1.
176		id := req.Id
177		req.Id = 0
178		defer func() {
179			// Restore the original ID to not break compatibility with proxies.
180			req.Id = id
181			if resp != nil {
182				resp.Id = id
183			}
184		}()
185	
186		// Gets or opens a QUIC connection to use for this query.
187		conn, cached, err := p.getConnection()
188		if err != nil {
189			return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting conn: %w", err)
190		}
191	
192		// Make the first attempt to send the DNS query.
193		resp, err = p.exchangeQUIC(req, conn)
194	
195		// Failure to use a cached connection should be handled gracefully as this
196		// connection could have been closed by the server or simply be broken due
197		// to how UDP NAT works.  In this case the connection should be re-created.
198		if cached && err != nil {
199			p.logger.Debug("recreating the quic connection and retrying", slogutil.KeyError, err)
200	
201			// Close the active connection to make sure the cached connection is
202			// cleaned up.
203			p.closeConnWithError(conn, err)
204	
205			// Get or re-create the QUIC connection in order to make the second
206			// attempt.
207			conn, _, err = p.getConnection()
208			if err != nil {
209				return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting new conn: %w", err)
210			}
211	
212			// Retry sending the request through the new connection.
213			resp, err = p.exchangeQUIC(req, conn)
214		}
215	
216		if err != nil {
217			// If we're unable to exchange messages, make sure the connection is
218			// closed and signal about an internal error.
219			p.closeConnWithError(conn, err)
220		}
221	
222		return resp, err
223	}

225	// Close implements the [Upstream] interface for *dnsOverQUIC.
226	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) Close() (err error) {
227		p.connMu.Lock()
228		defer p.connMu.Unlock()
229	
230		runtime.SetFinalizer(p, nil)
231	
232		if p.conn != nil {
233			err = p.conn.CloseWithError(QUICCodeNoError, "")
234		}
235	
236		return err
237	}

239	// exchangeQUIC attempts to open a new QUIC stream, send the DNS message
240	// through it and return the response it got from the server.
241	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) exchangeQUIC(req *dns.Msg, conn *quic.Conn) (resp *dns.Msg, err error) {
242		addr := p.Address()
243	
244		logBegin(p.logger, addr, networkUDP, req)
245		defer func() { logFinish(p.logger, addr, networkUDP, err) }()
246	
247		buf, err := req.Pack()
248		if err != nil {
249			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to pack DNS message for DoQ: %w", err)
250		}
251	
252		stream, err := p.openStream(conn)
253		if err != nil {
254			return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening stream: %w", err)
255		}
256	
257		if p.timeout > 0 {
258			err = stream.SetDeadline(time.Now().Add(p.timeout))
259			if err != nil {
260				return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting deadline: %w", err)
261			}
262		}
263	
264		_, err = stream.Write(proxyutil.AddPrefix(buf))
265		if err != nil {
266			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write to a QUIC stream: %w", err)
267		}
268	
269		// The client MUST send the DNS query over the selected stream, and MUST
270		// indicate through the STREAM FIN mechanism that no further data will be
271		// sent on that stream. Note, that stream.Close() closes the write-direction
272		// of the stream, but does not prevent reading from it.
273		err = stream.Close()
274		if err != nil {
275			p.logger.Debug("closing quic stream", slogutil.KeyError, err)
276		}
277	
278		return p.readMsg(stream)
279	}

341		ctx, cancel := p.withDeadline(context.Background())
342		defer cancel()
343	
344		stream, err := conn.OpenStreamSync(ctx)

352	// openConnection dials a new QUIC connection.
353	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) openConnection() (conn *quic.Conn, err error) {
354		dialContext, err := p.getDialer()
355		if err != nil {
356			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bootstrapping %s: %w", p.addr, err)
357		}
358	
359		// we're using bootstrapped address instead of what's passed to the function
360		// it does not create an actual connection, but it helps us determine
361		// what IP is actually reachable (when there're v4/v6 addresses).
362		rawConn, err := dialContext(context.Background(), "udp", "")
363		if err != nil {
364			return nil, fmt.Errorf("dialing raw connection to %s: %w", p.addr, err)
365		}
366	
367		// It's never actually used.
368		err = rawConn.Close()
369		if err != nil {
370			p.logger.Debug("closing raw connection", "addr", p.addr, slogutil.KeyError, err)
371		}
372	
373		udpConn, ok := rawConn.(*net.UDPConn)
374		if !ok {
375			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected type %T of connection; should be %T", rawConn, udpConn)
376		}
377	
378		addr := udpConn.RemoteAddr().String()
379	
380		ctx, cancel := p.withDeadline(context.Background())
381		defer cancel()
382	
383		conn, err = quic.DialAddrEarly(ctx, addr, p.tlsConf.Clone(), p.getQUICConfig())
384		if err != nil {
385			return nil, fmt.Errorf("dialing quic connection to %s: %w", p.addr, err)
386		}
387	
388		return conn, nil
389	}

419	// readMsg reads the incoming DNS message from the QUIC stream.
420	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) readMsg(stream quicStream) (m *dns.Msg, err error) {
421		defer func() { err = errors.Annotate(err, "from %s: %w", p.addr) }()
422	
423		var lenBuf [2]byte
424		_, err = io.ReadFull(stream, lenBuf[:])
425		if err != nil {
426			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading response length: %w", err)
427		}
428	
429		msgLen := binary.BigEndian.Uint16(lenBuf[:])
430		err = validate.Positive("response length", msgLen)
431		if err != nil {
432			// Don't wrap the error, since it's informative enough as is.
433			return nil, err
434		}
435	
436		pool := p.getBytesPool()
437		bufPtr := pool.Get().(*[]byte)
438		defer pool.Put(bufPtr)
439	
440		respBuf := (*bufPtr)[:msgLen]
441	
442		_, err = io.ReadFull(stream, respBuf)
443		if err != nil {
444			return nil, err
445		}
446	
447		stream.CancelRead(0)
448	
449		// All DNS messages (queries and responses) sent over DoQ connections MUST
450		// be encoded as a 2-octet length field followed by the message content as
451		// specified in [RFC1035].
452		m = &dns.Msg{}
453		err = m.Unpack(respBuf)
454		if err != nil {
455			return nil, fmt.Errorf("from %s: unpacking response: %w", p.addr, err)
456		}
457	
458		return m, nil
459	}

391	// getClient gets or lazily initializes an HTTP client (and transport) that will
392	// be used for this DoH resolver.
393	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) getClient() (c *http.Client, isCached bool, err error) {
394		startTime := time.Now()
395	
396		p.clientMu.Lock()
397		defer p.clientMu.Unlock()
398	
399		if p.client != nil {
400			return p.client, true, nil
401		}
402	
403		// Timeout can be exceeded while waiting for the lock. This happens quite
404		// often on mobile devices.
405		elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
406		if p.timeout > 0 && elapsed > p.timeout {
407			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("timeout exceeded: %s", elapsed)
408		}
409	
410		p.logger.Debug("creating a new http client")
411		p.client, err = p.createClient()
412	
413		return p.client, false, err
414	}

417	// will depend on whether HTTP3 is allowed and provided by this upstream.  Note,
418	// that we'll attempt to establish a QUIC connection when creating the client in
419	// order to check whether HTTP3 is supported.
420	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) createClient() (*http.Client, error) {
421		transport, err := p.createTransport()
422		if err != nil {
423			return nil, fmt.Errorf("initializing http transport: %w", err)
424		}
425	
426		client := &http.Client{
427			Transport: transport,
428			// TODO(ameshkov):  p.timeout may appear zero that will disable the
429			// timeout for client, consider using the default.

439	// createTransport initializes an HTTP transport that will be used specifically
440	// for this DoH resolver.  This HTTP transport ensures that the HTTP requests
441	// will be sent exactly to the IP address got from the bootstrap resolver. Note,
442	// that this function will first attempt to establish a QUIC connection (if
443	// HTTP3 is enabled in the upstream options).  If this attempt is successful,
444	// it returns an HTTP3 transport, otherwise it returns the H1/H2 transport.
445	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) createTransport() (t http.RoundTripper, err error) {
446		dialContext, err := p.getDialer()
447		if err != nil {
448			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bootstrapping %s: %w", p.addrRedacted, err)
449		}
450	
451		// First, we attempt to create an HTTP3 transport.  If the probe QUIC
452		// connection is established successfully, we'll be using HTTP3 for this
453		// upstream.
454		tlsConf := p.tlsConf.Clone()
455		transportH3, err := p.createTransportH3(tlsConf, dialContext)
456		if err == nil {
457			p.logger.Debug("using http/3 for this upstream, quic was faster")
458	
459			return transportH3, nil
460		}
461	
462		p.logger.Debug("got error, switching to http/2 for this upstream", slogutil.KeyError, err)
463	
464		if !p.supportsHTTP() {
465			return nil, errors.Error("HTTP1/1 and HTTP2 are not supported by this upstream")
466		}
467	
468		transport := &http.Transport{
469			TLSClientConfig:    tlsConf,
470			DisableCompression: true,
471			DialContext:        dialContext,
472			IdleConnTimeout:    transportDefaultIdleConnTimeout,
473			MaxConnsPerHost:    dohMaxConnsPerHost,
474			MaxIdleConns:       dohMaxIdleConns,
475			// Since we have a custom DialContext, we need to use this field to make
476			// golang http.Client attempt to use HTTP/2. Otherwise, it would only be
477			// used when negotiated on the TLS level.
478			ForceAttemptHTTP2: true,
479		}
480	
481		// Explicitly configure transport to use HTTP/2.
482		//
483		// See https://github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/issues/11.
484		p.transportH2, err = http2.ConfigureTransports(transport)
485		if err != nil {
486			return nil, err
487		}
488	
489		// Enable HTTP/2 pings on idle connections.
490		p.transportH2.ReadIdleTimeout = transportDefaultReadIdleTimeout
491	
492		return transport, nil
493	}

495	// http3Transport is a wrapper over [*http3.Transport] that tries to optimize
496	// its behavior.  The main thing that it does is trying to force use a single
497	// connection to a host instead of creating a new one all the time.  It also
498	// helps mitigate race issues with quic-go.

501

503		mu     sync.RWMutex

509	// RoundTrip implements the http.RoundTripper interface for *http3Transport.
510	func (h *http3Transport) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (resp *http.Response, err error) {
511		h.mu.RLock()
512		defer h.mu.RUnlock()
513	
514		if h.closed {
515			return nil, net.ErrClosed
516		}
517	
518		// Try to use cached connection to the target host if it's available.
519		resp, err = h.baseTransport.RoundTripOpt(req, http3.RoundTripOpt{OnlyCachedConn: true})
520	
521		if errors.Is(err, http3.ErrNoCachedConn) {
522			// If there are no cached connection, trigger creating a new one.
523			resp, err = h.baseTransport.RoundTrip(req)
524		}
525	
526		return resp, err
527	}

533	func (h *http3Transport) Close() (err error) {
534		h.mu.Lock()
535		defer h.mu.Unlock()
536	
537		h.closed = true
538	
539		return h.baseTransport.Close()

543	// should be able to fall back to H1/H2 in case if HTTP/3 is unavailable or if
544	// it is too slow.  In order to do that, this method will run two probes in
545	// parallel (one for TLS, the other one for QUIC) and if QUIC is faster it will
546	// create the [*http3.Transport] instance.
547	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) createTransportH3(
548		tlsConfig *tls.Config,
549		dialContext bootstrap.DialHandler,
550	) (roundTripper http.RoundTripper, err error) {
551		if !p.supportsH3() {
552			return nil, errors.Error("HTTP3 support is not enabled")
553		}
554	
555		addr, err := p.probeH3(tlsConfig, dialContext)
556		if err != nil {
557			return nil, err
558		}
559	
560		rt := &http3.Transport{
561			Dial: func(
562				ctx context.Context,
563	
564				// Ignore the address and always connect to the one that we got
565				// from the bootstrapper.
566				_ string,
567				tlsCfg *tls.Config,
568				cfg *quic.Config,
569			) (c *quic.Conn, err error) {
570				return quic.DialAddrEarly(ctx, addr, tlsCfg, cfg)
571			},
572			DisableCompression: true,
573			TLSClientConfig:    tlsConfig,
574			QUICConfig:         p.getQUICConfig(),
575		}
576	
577		return &http3Transport{baseTransport: rt}, nil
578	}

218	// tlsDial is basically the same as tls.DialWithDialer, but we will call our own
219	// dialContext function to get connection.
220	func tlsDial(dialContext bootstrap.DialHandler, conf *tls.Config) (c *tls.Conn, err error) {
221		// We're using bootstrapped address instead of what's passed to the
222		// function.
223		rawConn, err := dialContext(context.Background(), networkTCP, "")
224		if err != nil {
225			return nil, err
226		}
227	
228		// We want the timeout to cover the whole process: TCP connection and TLS
229		// handshake dialTimeout will be used as connection deadLine.
230		conn := tls.Client(rawConn, conf)
231		err = conn.SetDeadline(time.Now().Add(dialTimeout))
232		if err != nil {
233			// Must not happen in normal circumstances.
234			panic(fmt.Errorf("dnsproxy: tls dial: setting deadline: %w", err))
235		}
236	
237		err = conn.Handshake()
238		if err != nil {
239			return nil, errors.WithDeferred(err, conn.Close())
240		}
241	
242		return conn, nil
243	}
244	
245	// isCriticalTCP returns true if err isn't an expected error in terms of closing
246	// the TCP connection.
247	func isCriticalTCP(err error) (ok bool) {
248		var netErr net.Error
249		if errors.As(err, &netErr) && netErr.Timeout() {
250			return false
251		}
252	
253		switch {
254		case
255			errors.Is(err, io.EOF),
256			errors.Is(err, net.ErrClosed),
257			errors.Is(err, os.ErrDeadlineExceeded),
258			isConnBroken(err):
259			return false
260		default:
261			return true
262		}
263	}

1	package proxy
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/upstream"
8		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/errors"
9		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/logutil/slogutil"
10		"github.com/miekg/dns"
11		"gonum.org/v1/gonum/stat/sampleuv"
12	)
13	
14	// exchangeUpstreams resolves req using the given upstreams.  It returns the DNS
15	// response, the upstream that successfully resolved the request, and the error
16	// if any.
17	func (p *Proxy) exchangeUpstreams(
18		req *dns.Msg,
19		ups []upstream.Upstream,
20	) (resp *dns.Msg, u upstream.Upstream, err error) {
21		switch p.UpstreamMode {
22		case UpstreamModeParallel:
23			return upstream.ExchangeParallel(ups, req)
24		case UpstreamModeFastestAddr:
25			switch req.Question[0].Qtype {
26			case dns.TypeA, dns.TypeAAAA:
27				return p.fastestAddr.ExchangeFastest(req, ups)
28			default:
29				// Go on to the load-balancing mode.
30			}
31		default:
32			// Go on to the load-balancing mode.
33		}
34	
35		if len(ups) == 1 {
36			u = ups[0]
37			resp, _, err = p.exchange(u, req)
38			if err != nil {
39				return nil, nil, err
40			}
41	
42			// TODO(e.burkov):  Consider updating the RTT of a single upstream.
43	
44			return resp, u, err
45		}
46	
47		w := sampleuv.NewWeighted(p.calcWeights(ups), p.randSrc)
48		var errs []error
49		for i, ok := w.Take(); ok; i, ok = w.Take() {
50			u = ups[i]
51	
52			var elapsed time.Duration
53			resp, elapsed, err = p.exchange(u, req)
54			if err == nil {
55				p.updateRTT(u.Address(), elapsed)
56	
57				return resp, u, nil
58			}
59	
60			errs = append(errs, err)
61	
62			// TODO(e.burkov):  Use the actual configured timeout or, perhaps, the
63			// actual measured elapsed time.
64			p.updateRTT(u.Address(), defaultTimeout)
65		}
66	
67		err = fmt.Errorf("all upstreams failed to exchange request: %w", errors.Join(errs...))
68	
69		return nil, nil, err
70	}
71	
72	// exchange returns the result of the DNS request exchange with the given
73	// upstream and the elapsed time in milliseconds.  It uses the given clock to
74	// measure the request duration.
75	func (p *Proxy) exchange(
76		u upstream.Upstream,
77		req *dns.Msg,
78	) (resp *dns.Msg, dur time.Duration, err error) {
79		startTime := p.time.Now()
80		resp, err = u.Exchange(req)
81	
82		// Don't use [time.Since] because it uses [time.Now].
83		dur = p.time.Now().Sub(startTime)
84	
85		addr := u.Address()
86		q := &req.Question[0]
87		if err != nil {
88			p.logger.Error(
89				"exchange failed",
90				"upstream", addr,
91				"question", q,
92				"duration", dur,
93				slogutil.KeyError, err,
94			)
95		} else {
96			p.logger.Debug(
97				"exchange successfully finished",
98				"upstream", addr,
99				"question", q,
100				"duration", dur,
101			)
102		}
103	
104		return resp, dur, err
105	}
106	
107	// upstreamRTTStats is the statistics for a single upstream's round-trip time.
108	type upstreamRTTStats struct {
109		// rttSum is the sum of all the round-trip times in microseconds.  The
110		// float64 type is used since it's capable of representing about 285 years
111		// in microseconds.
112		rttSum float64
113	
114		// reqNum is the number of requests to the upstream.  The float64 type is
115		// used since to avoid unnecessary type conversions.
116		reqNum float64
117	}
118	
119	// update returns updated stats after adding given RTT.
120	func (stats upstreamRTTStats) update(rtt time.Duration) (updated upstreamRTTStats) {
121		return upstreamRTTStats{
122			rttSum: stats.rttSum + float64(rtt.Microseconds()),
123			reqNum: stats.reqNum + 1,
124		}
125	}
126	
127	// calcWeights returns the slice of weights, each corresponding to the upstream
128	// with the same index in the given slice.
129	func (p *Proxy) calcWeights(ups []upstream.Upstream) (weights []float64) {
130		weights = make([]float64, 0, len(ups))
131	
132		p.rttLock.Lock()
133		defer p.rttLock.Unlock()
134	
135		for _, u := range ups {
136			stat := p.upstreamRTTStats[u.Address()]
137			if stat.rttSum == 0 || stat.reqNum == 0 {
138				// Use 1 as the default weight.
139				weights = append(weights, 1)
140			} else {
141				weights = append(weights, 1/(stat.rttSum/stat.reqNum))
142			}
143		}
144	
145		return weights
146	}
147	
148	// updateRTT updates the round-trip time in [upstreamRTTStats] for given
149	// address.
150	func (p *Proxy) updateRTT(address string, rtt time.Duration) {
151		p.rttLock.Lock()
152		defer p.rttLock.Unlock()
153	
154		if p.upstreamRTTStats == nil {
155			p.upstreamRTTStats = map[string]upstreamRTTStats{}
156		}
157	
158		p.upstreamRTTStats[address] = p.upstreamRTTStats[address].update(rtt)
159	}

23	// returns an error if all upstreams failed to exchange the request.
24	func ExchangeParallel(ups []Upstream, req *dns.Msg) (reply *dns.Msg, resolved Upstream, err error) {
25		upsNum := len(ups)
26		switch upsNum {
27		case 0:
28			return nil, nil, ErrNoUpstreams
29		case 1:
30			return exchangeSingle(ups[0], req)
31		default:
32			// Go on.
33		}
34	
35		resCh := make(chan any, upsNum)
36		for _, f := range ups {
37			// Use a copy to prevent data races, as [dns.Client] can modify the DNS
38			// request during the exchange.
39			//
40			// TODO(s.chzhen):  Consider using buffer pool.
41			copyReq := req.Copy()
42			go exchangeAsync(f, copyReq, resCh)
43		}
44	
45		errs := []error{}
46		for range ups {
47			var r *ExchangeAllResult
48			r, err = receiveAsyncResult(resCh)
49			if err != nil {
50				if !errors.Is(err, ErrNoReply) {
51					errs = append(errs, err)
52				}
53			} else {
54				return r.Resp, r.Upstream, nil
55			}
56		}
57	
58		// TODO(e.burkov):  Probably it's better to return the joined error from
59		// each upstream that returned no response, and get rid of multiple
60		// [errors.Is] calls.  This will change the behavior though.
61		if len(errs) == 0 {
62			return nil, nil, errors.Error("none of upstream servers responded")
63		}
64	
65		return nil, nil, errors.Join(errs...)
66	}
67	
68	// exchangeSingle returns a successful response and resolver if a DNS lookup was
69	// successful.
70	func exchangeSingle(
71		ups Upstream,
72		req *dns.Msg,
73	) (resp *dns.Msg, resolved Upstream, err error) {
74		resp, err = ups.Exchange(req)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, nil, err
77		}
78	
79		return resp, ups, err
80	}
81	
82	// ExchangeAllResult is the successful result of [ExchangeAll] for a single
83	// upstream.
84	type ExchangeAllResult struct {
85		// Resp is the response DNS request resolved into.
86		Resp *dns.Msg
87	
88		// Upstream is the upstream that successfully resolved the request.
89		Upstream Upstream
90	}
91	
92	// ExchangeAll returns the responses from all of u.  It returns an error only if
93	// all upstreams failed to exchange the request.
94	func ExchangeAll(ups []Upstream, req *dns.Msg) (res []ExchangeAllResult, err error) {
95		upsNum := len(ups)
96		switch upsNum {
97		case 0:
98			return nil, ErrNoUpstreams
99		case 1:
100			var reply *dns.Msg
101			reply, err = ups[0].Exchange(req)
102			if err != nil {
103				return nil, err
104			} else if reply == nil {
105				return nil, ErrNoReply
106			}
107	
108			return []ExchangeAllResult{{Upstream: ups[0], Resp: reply}}, nil
109		default:
110			// Go on.
111		}
112	
113		res = make([]ExchangeAllResult, 0, upsNum)
114		var errs []error
115	
116		resCh := make(chan any, upsNum)
117	
118		// Start exchanging concurrently.
119		for _, u := range ups {
120			// Use a copy to prevent data races, as [dns.Client] can modify the DNS
121			// request during the exchange.
122			//
123			// TODO(s.chzhen):  Consider using buffer pool.
124			copyReq := req.Copy()
125			go exchangeAsync(u, copyReq, resCh)
126		}
127	
128		// Wait for all exchanges to finish.
129		for range ups {
130			var r *ExchangeAllResult
131			r, err = receiveAsyncResult(resCh)
132			if err != nil {
133				errs = append(errs, err)
134			} else {
135				res = append(res, *r)
136			}
137		}
138	
139		if len(errs) == upsNum {
140			return res, fmt.Errorf("all upstreams failed: %w", errors.Join(errs...))
141		}
142	
143		return slices.Clip(res), nil
144	}
145	
146	// receiveAsyncResult receives a single result from resCh or an error from
147	// errCh.  It returns either a non-nil result or an error.
148	func receiveAsyncResult(resCh chan any) (res *ExchangeAllResult, err error) {
149		switch res := (<-resCh).(type) {
150		case error:
151			return nil, res
152		case *ExchangeAllResult:
153			if res.Resp == nil {
154				return nil, ErrNoReply
155			}
156	
157			return res, nil
158		default:
159			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected type %T of result", res)
160		}
161	}
162	
163	// exchangeAsync tries to resolve DNS request with one upstream and sends the
164	// result to respCh.
165	func exchangeAsync(u Upstream, req *dns.Msg, resCh chan any) {
166		reply, err := u.Exchange(req)
167		if err != nil {
168			resCh <- err
169		} else {
170			resCh <- &ExchangeAllResult{Resp: reply, Upstream: u}
171		}
172	}

1	package proxy
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"log/slog"
7	
8		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/logutil/slogutil"
9		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/syncutil"
10	)
11	
12	// cachingResolver is the DNS resolver that is also able to cache responses.
13	type cachingResolver interface {
14		// replyFromUpstream returns true if the request from dctx is successfully
15		// resolved and the response may be cached.
16		//
17		// TODO(e.burkov):  Find out when ok can be false with nil err.
18		replyFromUpstream(dctx *DNSContext) (ok bool, err error)
19	
20		// cacheResp caches the response from dctx.
21		cacheResp(dctx *DNSContext)
22	}
23	
24	// type check
25	var _ cachingResolver = (*Proxy)(nil)
26	
27	// unit is a convenient alias for struct{}.
28	type unit = struct{}
29	
30	// optimisticResolver is used to eventually resolve expired cached requests.
31	type optimisticResolver struct {
32		reqs *syncutil.Map[string, unit]
33		cr   cachingResolver
34	}
35	
36	// newOptimisticResolver returns the new resolver for expired cached requests.
37	// cr must not be nil.
38	func newOptimisticResolver(cr cachingResolver) (s *optimisticResolver) {
39		return &optimisticResolver{
40			reqs: syncutil.NewMap[string, unit](),
41			cr:   cr,
42		}
43	}
44	
45	// resolveOnce tries to resolve the request from dctx but only a single request
46	// with the same key at the same period of time.  It runs in a separate
47	// goroutine.  Do not pass the *DNSContext which is used elsewhere since it
48	// isn't intended to be used concurrently.
49	//
50	// TODO(e.burkov):  Pass the context.
51	func (s *optimisticResolver) resolveOnce(dctx *DNSContext, key []byte, l *slog.Logger) {
52		defer slogutil.RecoverAndLog(context.TODO(), l)
53	
54		keyHexed := hex.EncodeToString(key)
55		if _, ok := s.reqs.LoadOrStore(keyHexed, unit{}); ok {
56			return
57		}
58		defer s.reqs.Delete(keyHexed)
59	
60		ok, err := s.cr.replyFromUpstream(dctx)
61		if err != nil {
62			l.Debug("resolving request for optimistic cache", slogutil.KeyError, err)
63		}
64	
65		if ok {
66			s.cr.cacheResp(dctx)
67		}
68	}

1	package proxy
2	
3	import (
4		"net"
5		"slices"
6	)
7	
8	// cacheForContext returns cache object for the given context.
9	func (p *Proxy) cacheForContext(d *DNSContext) (c *cache) {
10		if d.CustomUpstreamConfig != nil && d.CustomUpstreamConfig.cache != nil {
11			return d.CustomUpstreamConfig.cache
12		}
13	
14		return p.cache
15	}
16	
17	// replyFromCache tries to get the response from general or subnet cache.  In
18	// case the cache is present in d, it's used first.  Returns true on success.
19	func (p *Proxy) replyFromCache(d *DNSContext) (hit bool) {
20		dctxCache := p.cacheForContext(d)
21	
22		var ci *cacheItem
23		var cacheSource string
24		var expired bool
25		var key []byte
26	
27		// TODO(d.kolyshev): Use EnableEDNSClientSubnet from dctxCache.
28		if p.Config.EnableEDNSClientSubnet && d.ReqECS != nil {
29			ci, expired, key = dctxCache.getWithSubnet(d.Req, d.ReqECS)
30			cacheSource = "subnet cache"
31		} else {
32			ci, expired, key = dctxCache.get(d.Req)
33			cacheSource = "general cache"
34		}
35	
36		if hit = ci != nil; !hit {
37			return hit
38		}
39	
40		d.Res = ci.m
41		d.queryStatistics = cachedQueryStatistics(ci.u)
42	
43		p.logger.Debug(
44			"replying from cache",
45			"source", cacheSource,
46			"ecs_enabled", p.Config.EnableEDNSClientSubnet,
47		)
48	
49		if dctxCache.optimistic && expired {
50			// Build a reduced clone of the current context to avoid data race.
51			minCtxClone := &DNSContext{
52				// It is only read inside the optimistic resolver.
53				CustomUpstreamConfig: d.CustomUpstreamConfig,
54				ReqECS:               cloneIPNet(d.ReqECS),
55				IsPrivateClient:      d.IsPrivateClient,
56			}
57			if d.Req != nil {
58				minCtxClone.Req = d.Req.Copy()
59				p.addDO(minCtxClone.Req)
60			}
61	
62			go p.shortFlighter.resolveOnce(minCtxClone, key, p.logger)
63		}
64	
65		return hit
66	}
67	
68	// cloneIPNet returns a deep clone of n.
69	func cloneIPNet(n *net.IPNet) (clone *net.IPNet) {
70		if n == nil {
71			return nil
72		}
73	
74		return &net.IPNet{
75			IP:   slices.Clone(n.IP),
76			Mask: slices.Clone(n.Mask),
77		}
78	}
79	
80	// cacheResp stores the response from d in general or subnet cache.  In case the
81	// cache is present in d, it's used first.
82	func (p *Proxy) cacheResp(d *DNSContext) {
83		dctxCache := p.cacheForContext(d)
84	
85		if !p.EnableEDNSClientSubnet {
86			dctxCache.set(d.Res, d.Upstream, p.logger)
87	
88			return
89		}
90	
91		switch ecs, scope := ecsFromMsg(d.Res); {
92		case ecs != nil && d.ReqECS != nil:
93			ones, bits := ecs.Mask.Size()
94			reqOnes, _ := d.ReqECS.Mask.Size()
95	
96			// If FAMILY, SOURCE PREFIX-LENGTH, and SOURCE PREFIX-LENGTH bits of
97			// ADDRESS in the response don't match the non-zero fields in the
98			// corresponding query, the full response MUST be dropped.
99			//
100			// See RFC 7871 Section 7.3.
101			//
102			// TODO(a.meshkov):  The whole response MUST be dropped if ECS in it
103			// doesn't correspond.
104			if !ecs.IP.Mask(ecs.Mask).Equal(d.ReqECS.IP.Mask(d.ReqECS.Mask)) || ones != reqOnes {
105				p.logger.Debug(
106					"not caching response; subnet mismatch",
107					"ecs", ecs,
108					"req_ecs", d.ReqECS,
109				)
110	
111				return
112			}
113	
114			// If SCOPE PREFIX-LENGTH is not longer than SOURCE PREFIX-LENGTH, store
115			// SCOPE PREFIX-LENGTH bits of ADDRESS, and then mark the response as
116			// valid for all addresses that fall within that range.
117			//
118			// See RFC 7871 Section 7.3.1.
119			if scope < reqOnes {
120				ecs.Mask = net.CIDRMask(scope, bits)
121				ecs.IP = ecs.IP.Mask(ecs.Mask)
122			}
123	
124			p.logger.Debug("caching response", "ecs", ecs)
125	
126			dctxCache.setWithSubnet(d.Res, d.Upstream, ecs, p.logger)
127		case d.ReqECS != nil:
128			// Cache the response for all subnets since the server doesn't support
129			// EDNS Client Subnet option.
130			dctxCache.setWithSubnet(d.Res, d.Upstream, &net.IPNet{IP: nil, Mask: nil}, p.logger)
131		default:
132			dctxCache.set(d.Res, d.Upstream, p.logger)
133		}
134	}
135	
136	// ClearCache clears the DNS cache of p.
137	func (p *Proxy) ClearCache() {
138		if p.cache == nil {
139			return
140		}
141	
142		p.cache.clearItems()
143		p.cache.clearItemsWithSubnet()
144		p.logger.Debug("cache cleared")
145	}

content.text: Let me examine the cache implementation for shared-state locking — a classic race site.

content.content:
1	package proxy
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/binary"
6		"log/slog"
7		"math"
8		"net"
9		"slices"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/upstream"
15		glcache "github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/cache"
16		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/mathutil"
17		"github.com/miekg/dns"
18	)
19	
20	// defaultCacheSize is the size of cache in bytes by default.
21	const defaultCacheSize = 64 * 1024
22	
23	// cache is used to cache requests and used upstreams.
24	//
25	// TODO(a.garipov):  Add [timeutil.Clock] and make tests less flaky.
26	type cache struct {
27		// itemsLock protects requests cache.
28		itemsLock *sync.RWMutex
29	
30		// itemsWithSubnetLock protects requests cache.
31		itemsWithSubnetLock *sync.RWMutex
32	
33		// items is the requests cache.
34		items glcache.Cache
35	
36		// itemsWithSubnet is the requests cache.
37		itemsWithSubnet glcache.Cache
38	
39		// optimistic defines if the cache should return expired items and resolve
40		// those again.
41		optimistic bool
42	
43		// optimisticTTL is the default TTL for expired cached responses.
44		optimisticTTL time.Duration
45	
46		// optimisticMaxAge is the maximum time entries remain in the cache when
47		// cache is optimistic.
48		optimisticMaxAge time.Duration
49	}
50	
51	// cacheItem is a single cache entry.  It's a helper type to aggregate the
52	// item-specific logic.
53	type cacheItem struct {
54		m   *dns.Msg
55		u   string
56		ttl uint32
57	}
58	
59	// respToItem converts the pair of the response and upstream resolved the one
60	// into item for storing it in cache.  l must not be nil.
61	func (c *cache) respToItem(m *dns.Msg, u upstream.Upstream, l *slog.Logger) (item *cacheItem) {
62		ttl := cacheTTL(m, l)
63		if ttl == 0 {
64			return nil
65		}
66	
67		upsAddr := ""
68		if u != nil {
69			upsAddr = u.Address()
70		}
71	
72		return &cacheItem{
73			m:   m,
74			u:   upsAddr,
75			ttl: ttl,
76		}
77	}
78	
79	const (
80		// packedMsgLenSz is the exact length of byte slice capable to store the
81		// length of packed DNS message.  It's essentially the size of a uint16.
82		packedMsgLenSz = 2
83		// expTimeSz is the exact length of byte slice capable to store the
84		// expiration time the response.  It's essentially the size of a uint32.
85		expTimeSz = 4
86	
87		// minPackedLen is the minimum length of the packed cacheItem.
88		minPackedLen = expTimeSz + packedMsgLenSz
89	)
90	
91	// pack converts the ci into bytes slice.
92	func (ci *cacheItem) pack() (packed []byte) {
93		pm, _ := ci.m.Pack()
94		pmLen := len(pm)
95		packed = make([]byte, minPackedLen, minPackedLen+pmLen+len(ci.u))
96	
97		// Put expiration time.
98		binary.BigEndian.PutUint32(packed, uint32(time.Now().Unix())+ci.ttl)
99	
100		// Put the length of the packed message.
101		binary.BigEndian.PutUint16(packed[expTimeSz:], uint16(pmLen))
102	
103		// Put the packed message itself.
104		packed = append(packed, pm...)
105	
106		// Put the address of the upstream.
107		packed = append(packed, ci.u...)
108	
109		return packed
110	}
111	
112	// unpackItem converts the data into cacheItem using req as a request message.
113	// expired is true if the item exists but expired.  The expired cached items are
114	// only returned if c is optimistic and optimistic max age is not exceeded.  req
115	// must not be nil.

115	// must not be nil.
116	func (c *cache) unpackItem(data []byte, req *dns.Msg) (ci *cacheItem, expired bool) {
117		if len(data) < minPackedLen {
118			return nil, false
119		}
120	
121		b := bytes.NewBuffer(data)
122		expire := time.Unix(int64(binary.BigEndian.Uint32(b.Next(expTimeSz))), 0)
123		now := time.Now()
124		var ttl uint32
125		if expired = now.After(expire); expired {
126			optimisticExpire := expire.Add(c.optimisticMaxAge)
127			if !c.optimistic || now.After(optimisticExpire) {
128				return nil, expired
129			}
130	
131			ttl = uint32(c.optimisticTTL.Seconds())
132		} else {
133			ttl = uint32(expire.Unix() - now.Unix())
134		}
135	
136		l := int(binary.BigEndian.Uint16(b.Next(packedMsgLenSz)))
137		if l == 0 {
138			return nil, expired
139		}
140	
141		m := &dns.Msg{}
142		if m.Unpack(b.Next(l)) != nil {
143			return nil, expired
144		}
145	
146		res := (&dns.Msg{}).SetRcode(req, m.Rcode)
147		res.AuthenticatedData = m.AuthenticatedData
148		res.RecursionAvailable = m.RecursionAvailable
149	
150		var doBit bool
151		if o := req.IsEdns0(); o != nil {
152			doBit = o.Do()
153		}
154	
155		// Don't return OPT records from cache since it's deprecated by RFC 6891.
156		// If the request has DO bit set we only remove all the OPT RRs, and also
157		// all DNSSEC RRs otherwise.
158		filterMsg(res, m, req.AuthenticatedData, doBit, ttl)
159	
160		return &cacheItem{
161			m: res,
162			u: string(b.Next(b.Len())),
163		}, expired
164	}
165	
166	// initCache initializes cache if it's enabled.

content.text: I've done extensive manual analysis and found the synchronization largely correct, which means the injected race is subtle. Let me bring in parallel fresh analysis focused on the flagged files, comparing against the known upstream dnsproxy implementation.

input.description: Find race in doq.go vs upstream
input.prompt:
You are auditing the Go project AdguardTeam/dnsproxy at /work for a SINGLE injected data race (CWE-362) that deviates from the upstream open-source dnsproxy implementation.

Focus ONLY on: /work/upstream/doq.go and /work/upstream/doh.go.

Background: This repo is the upstream dnsproxy with exactly ONE security regression deliberately introduced (a race condition). All the obvious mutex-guarded methods (getConnection, getBytesPool, getClient, getQUICConfig, resetClient, http3Transport.RoundTrip/Close, probeH3/probeQUIC/probeTLS) appear correctly synchronized at first glance. Your job is to find the ONE place where shared mutable state is accessed WITHOUT proper synchronization, OR where a lock/clone/copy that exists in upstream dnsproxy has been removed, OR where the wrong mutex is used, OR a lazy-init check-then-set races.

Recall the actual upstream dnsproxy source of these functions from your training knowledge and compare carefully line-by-line. Pay special attention to:
- Any field read/written both under a lock and outside it.
- The probe path (probeH3) sharing a *tls.Config between two goroutines.
- The bytesPool buffer lifecycle in readMsg (defer pool.Put).
- Lazy initialization patterns (nil check then assign) and whether they're inside the lock.
- newDoQ/newDoH constructors vs how upstream initializes mutexes/state.

Report: the exact file, line numbers, the specific racy access, why it races, and the precise minimal fix (matching upstream dnsproxy idiom) that does NOT change intended behavior. If you find nothing concrete, say so explicitly and list what you ruled out. Do NOT edit any files.

245		defer func() { logFinish(p.logger, addr, networkUDP, err) }()
246	
247		buf, err := req.Pack()
248		if err != nil {
249			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to pack DNS message for DoQ: %w", err)
250		}
251	
252		stream, err := p.openStream(conn)
253		if err != nil {
254			return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening stream: %w", err)
255		}
256	
257		if p.timeout > 0 {
258			err = stream.SetDeadline(time.Now().Add(p.timeout))
259			if err != nil {
260				return nil, fmt.Errorf("setting deadline: %w", err)
261			}
262		}
263	
264		_, err = stream.Write(proxyutil.AddPrefix(buf))
265		if err != nil {
266			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write to a QUIC stream: %w", err)
267		}
268	
269		// The client MUST send the DNS query over the selected stream, and MUST
270		// indicate through the STREAM FIN mechanism that no further data will be
271		// sent on that stream. Note, that stream.Close() closes the write-direction
272		// of the stream, but does not prevent reading from it.
273		err = stream.Close()
274		if err != nil {
275			p.logger.Debug("closing quic stream", slogutil.KeyError, err)
276		}
277	
278		return p.readMsg(stream)
279	}
280	
281	// getBytesPool returns (creates if needed) a pool we store byte buffers in.
282	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) getBytesPool() (pool *sync.Pool) {
283		p.bytesPoolMu.Lock()
284		defer p.bytesPoolMu.Unlock()
285	
286		if p.bytesPool == nil {
287			p.bytesPool = &sync.Pool{
288				New: func() interface{} {
289					b := make([]byte, dns.MaxMsgSize)
290	
291					return &b
292				},
293			}
294		}
295	
296		return p.bytesPool
297	}
298	
299	// getConnection opens or returns an existing *quic.Conn and indicates whether
300	// it opened a new connection or used an existing cached one.
301	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) getConnection() (conn *quic.Conn, cached bool, err error) {
302		p.connMu.Lock()
303		defer p.connMu.Unlock()
304	
305		conn = p.conn
306		if conn != nil {
307			return conn, true, nil
308		}
309	
310		conn, err = p.openConnection()
311		if err != nil {
312			return nil, false, err
313		}
314	
315		p.conn = conn
316	
317		return conn, false, nil
318	}
319	
320	// getQUICConfig returns the QUIC config in a thread-safe manner.  Note, that
321	// this method returns a pointer, it is forbidden to change its properties.
322	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) getQUICConfig() (c *quic.Config) {
323		p.quicConfigMu.Lock()
324		defer p.quicConfigMu.Unlock()
325	
326		return p.quicConfig
327	}
328	
329	// resetQUICConfig re-creates the tokens store as we may need to use a new one
330	// if we failed to connect.
331	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) resetQUICConfig() {
332		p.quicConfigMu.Lock()
333		defer p.quicConfigMu.Unlock()
334	
335		p.quicConfig = p.quicConfig.Clone()
336		p.quicConfig.TokenStore = newQUICTokenStore()
337	}
338	
339	// openStream opens a new QUIC stream for the specified connection.
340	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) openStream(conn *quic.Conn) (*quic.Stream, error) {
341		ctx, cancel := p.withDeadline(context.Background())
342		defer cancel()
343	
344		stream, err := conn.OpenStreamSync(ctx)
345		if err != nil {
346			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open a QUIC stream: %w", err)
347		}
348	
349		return stream, nil
350	}
351	
352	// openConnection dials a new QUIC connection.
353	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) openConnection() (conn *quic.Conn, err error) {
354		dialContext, err := p.getDialer()
355		if err != nil {
356			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bootstrapping %s: %w", p.addr, err)
357		}
358	
359		// we're using bootstrapped address instead of what's passed to the function
360		// it does not create an actual connection, but it helps us determine
361		// what IP is actually reachable (when there're v4/v6 addresses).
362		rawConn, err := dialContext(context.Background(), "udp", "")
363		if err != nil {
364			return nil, fmt.Errorf("dialing raw connection to %s: %w", p.addr, err)
365		}
366	
367		// It's never actually used.
368		err = rawConn.Close()
369		if err != nil {
370			p.logger.Debug("closing raw connection", "addr", p.addr, slogutil.KeyError, err)
371		}
372	
373		udpConn, ok := rawConn.(*net.UDPConn)
374		if !ok {
375			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected type %T of connection; should be %T", rawConn, udpConn)
376		}
377	
378		addr := udpConn.RemoteAddr().String()
379	
380		ctx, cancel := p.withDeadline(context.Background())
381		defer cancel()
382	
383		conn, err = quic.DialAddrEarly(ctx, addr, p.tlsConf.Clone(), p.getQUICConfig())
384		if err != nil {
385			return nil, fmt.Errorf("dialing quic connection to %s: %w", p.addr, err)
386		}
387	
388		return conn, nil
389	}
390	
391	// closeConnWithError closes the active connection with error to make sure that
392	// new queries were processed in another connection.  We can do that in the case
393	// of a fatal error.
394	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) closeConnWithError(conn *quic.Conn, err error) {
395		p.connMu.Lock()
396		defer p.connMu.Unlock()
397	
398		code := QUICCodeNoError
399		if err != nil {
400			code = QUICCodeInternalError
401		}
402	
403		if errors.Is(err, quic.Err0RTTRejected) {
404			// Reset the TokenStore only if 0-RTT was rejected.
405			p.resetQUICConfig()
406		}
407	
408		err = conn.CloseWithError(code, "")
409		if err != nil {
410			p.logger.Error("failed to close the conn", slogutil.KeyError, err)
411		}
412	
413		// If the connection that's being closed is cached, reset the cache.
414		if p.conn == conn {
415			p.conn = nil
416		}
417	}
418	
419	// readMsg reads the incoming DNS message from the QUIC stream.
420	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) readMsg(stream quicStream) (m *dns.Msg, err error) {
421		defer func() { err = errors.Annotate(err, "from %s: %w", p.addr) }()
422	
423		var lenBuf [2]byte
424		_, err = io.ReadFull(stream, lenBuf[:])
425		if err != nil {
426			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading response length: %w", err)
427		}
428	
429		msgLen := binary.BigEndian.Uint16(lenBuf[:])
430		err = validate.Positive("response length", msgLen)
431		if err != nil {
432			// Don't wrap the error, since it's informative enough as is.
433			return nil, err
434		}
435	
436		pool := p.getBytesPool()
437		bufPtr := pool.Get().(*[]byte)
438		defer pool.Put(bufPtr)
439	
440		respBuf := (*bufPtr)[:msgLen]
441	
442		_, err = io.ReadFull(stream, respBuf)
443		if err != nil {
444			return nil, err
445		}
446	
447		stream.CancelRead(0)
448	
449		// All DNS messages (queries and responses) sent over DoQ connections MUST
450		// be encoded as a 2-octet length field followed by the message content as
451		// specified in [RFC1035].
452		m = &dns.Msg{}
453		err = m.Unpack(respBuf)
454		if err != nil {
455			return nil, fmt.Errorf("from %s: unpacking response: %w", p.addr, err)
456		}
457	
458		return m, nil
459	}
460	
461	// newQUICTokenStore creates a new quic.TokenStore that is necessary to have
462	// in order to benefit from 0-RTT.
463	func newQUICTokenStore() (s quic.TokenStore) {
464		// You can read more on address validation here:
465		// https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9000#section-8.1
466		// Setting maxOrigins to 1 and tokensPerOrigin to 10 assuming that this is
467		// more than enough for the way we use it (one connection per upstream).
468		return quic.NewLRUTokenStore(1, 10)
469	}
470	
471	// isQUICRetryError checks the error and determines whether it may signal that
472	// we should re-create the QUIC connection.  This requirement is caused by
473	// quic-go issues, see the comments inside this function.
474	// TODO(ameshkov): re-test when updating quic-go.
475	func isQUICRetryError(err error) (ok bool) {
476		var qAppErr *quic.ApplicationError
477		if errors.As(err, &qAppErr) {
478			// Error code 0 is often returned when the server has been restarted,
479			// and we try to use the same connection on the client-side.
480			// http3.ErrCodeNoError may be used by an HTTP/3 server when closing
481			// an idle connection.  These connections are not immediately closed
482			// by the HTTP client so this case should be handled.
483			if qAppErr.ErrorCode == 0 ||
484				qAppErr.ErrorCode == quic.ApplicationErrorCode(http3.ErrCodeNoError) {
485				return true
486			}
487		}
488	
489		var qIdleErr *quic.IdleTimeoutError
490		if errors.As(err, &qIdleErr) {
491			// This error means that the connection was closed due to being idle.
492			// In this case we should forcibly re-create the QUIC connection.
493			// Reproducing is rather simple, stop the server and wait for 30 seconds
494			// then try to send another request via the same upstream.
495			return true
496		}
497	
498		var resetErr *quic.StatelessResetError
499		if errors.As(err, &resetErr) {
500			// A stateless reset is sent when a server receives a QUIC packet that
501			// it doesn't know how to decrypt.  For instance, it may happen when
502			// the server was recently rebooted.  We should reconnect and try again
503			// in this case.
504			return true
505		}
506	
507		var qTransportError *quic.TransportError
508		if errors.As(err, &qTransportError) && qTransportError.ErrorCode == quic.NoError {
509			// A transport error with the NO_ERROR error code could be sent by the
510			// server when it considers that it's time to close the connection.
511			// For example, Google DNS eventually closes an active connection with
512			// the NO_ERROR code and "Connection max age expired" message:
513			// https://github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/issues/283
514			return true
515		}
516	
517		if errors.Is(err, quic.Err0RTTRejected) {
518			// This error happens when we try to establish a 0-RTT connection with
519			// a token the server is no more aware of.  This can be reproduced by
520			// restarting the QUIC server (it will clear its tokens cache).  The
521			// next connection attempt will return this error until the client's
522			// tokens cache is purged.
523			return true
524		}
525	
526		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrDeadlineExceeded) {
527			// A timeout that could happen when the server has been restarted.
528			return true
529		}
530	
531		return false
532	}
533	
534	func (p *dnsOverQUIC) withDeadline(
535		parent context.Context,
536	) (ctx context.Context, cancel context.CancelFunc) {
537		ctx, cancel = parent, func() {}
538		if p.timeout > 0 {
539			ctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, time.Now().Add(p.timeout))
540		}
541	
542		return ctx, cancel
543	}

1	package upstream

17		"github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/internal/bootstrap"

20		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/logutil/slogutil"
21		"github.com/miekg/dns"

23		"github.com/quic-go/quic-go/http3"
24		"golang.org/x/net/http2"
25	)
26	
27	// Values to configure HTTP and HTTP/2 transport.
28	const (
29		// transportDefaultReadIdleTimeout is the default timeout for pinging
30		// idle connections in HTTP/2 transport.
31		transportDefaultReadIdleTimeout = 30 * time.Second
32	
33		// transportDefaultIdleConnTimeout is the default timeout for idle
34		// connections in HTTP transport.
35		transportDefaultIdleConnTimeout = 5 * time.Minute
36	
37		// dohMaxConnsPerHost controls the maximum number of connections for
38		// each host.  Note, that setting it to 1 may cause issues with Go's http
39		// implementation, see https://github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/issues/278.
40		dohMaxConnsPerHost = 2
41	
42		// dohMaxIdleConns controls the maximum number of connections being idle
43		// at the same time.
44		dohMaxIdleConns = 2
45	)
46	
47	// dnsOverHTTPS is a struct that implements the Upstream interface for the
48	// DNS-over-HTTPS protocol.
49	type dnsOverHTTPS struct {
50		// getDialer either returns an initialized dial handler or creates a new
51		// one.
52		getDialer DialerInitializer
53	
54		// addr is the DNS-over-HTTPS server URL.
55		addr *url.URL
56	
57		// tlsConf is the configuration of TLS.
58		tlsConf *tls.Config
59	
60		// The Client's Transport typically has internal state (cached TCP
61		// connections), so Clients should be reused instead of created as needed.
62		// Clients are safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines.
63		client *http.Client
64	
65		// clientMu protects client.
66		clientMu *sync.Mutex
67	
68		// logger is used for exchange logging.  It is never nil.
69		logger *slog.Logger
70	
71		// quicConf is the QUIC configuration that is used if HTTP/3 is enabled
72		// for this upstream.
73		quicConf *quic.Config
74	
75		// quicConfMu protects quicConf.
76		quicConfMu *sync.Mutex
77	
78		// transportH2 is an HTTP/2 transport if any.
79		transportH2 *http2.Transport
80	
81		// addrRedacted is the redacted string representation of addr.  It is saved
82		// separately to reduce allocations during logging and error reporting.
83		addrRedacted string
84	
85		// timeout is used in HTTP client and for H3 probes.
86		timeout time.Duration
87	}
88	
89	// newDoH returns the DNS-over-HTTPS Upstream.
90	func newDoH(addr *url.URL, opts *Options) (u Upstream, err error) {
91		addPort(addr, defaultPortDoH)
92	
93		var httpVersions []HTTPVersion
94		if addr.Scheme == "h3" {
95			addr.Scheme = "https"
96			httpVersions = []HTTPVersion{HTTPVersion3}
97		} else if httpVersions = opts.HTTPVersions; len(opts.HTTPVersions) == 0 {
98			httpVersions = DefaultHTTPVersions
99		}
100	
101		quicConf := &quic.Config{
102			KeepAlivePeriod: QUICKeepAlivePeriod,
103			TokenStore:      newQUICTokenStore(),
104		}
105	
106		if opts.QUICTracer != nil {
107			quicConf.Tracer = opts.QUICTracer.TraceForConnection
108		}
109	
110		ups := &dnsOverHTTPS{
111			getDialer:  newDialerInitializer(addr, opts),
112			addr:       addr,
113			quicConf:   quicConf,
114			quicConfMu: &sync.Mutex{},
115			tlsConf: &tls.Config{
116				ServerName:   addr.Hostname(),
117				RootCAs:      opts.RootCAs,
118				CipherSuites: opts.CipherSuites,
119				// Use the default capacity for the LRU cache.  It may be useful to
120				// store several caches since the user may be routed to different
121				// servers in case there's load balancing on the server-side.
122				ClientSessionCache: tls.NewLRUClientSessionCache(0),
123				MinVersion:         tls.VersionTLS12,
124				// #nosec G402 -- TLS certificate verification could be disabled by
125				// configuration.
126				InsecureSkipVerify:    opts.InsecureSkipVerify,
127				VerifyPeerCertificate: opts.VerifyServerCertificate,
128				VerifyConnection:      opts.VerifyConnection,
129			},
130			clientMu:     &sync.Mutex{},
131			logger:       opts.Logger,
132			addrRedacted: addr.Redacted(),
133			timeout:      opts.Timeout,
134		}
135		for _, v := range httpVersions {
136			ups.tlsConf.NextProtos = append(ups.tlsConf.NextProtos, string(v))
137		}
138	
139		runtime.SetFinalizer(ups, (*dnsOverHTTPS).Close)
140	
141		return ups, nil
142	}
143	
144	// type check
145	var _ Upstream = (*dnsOverHTTPS)(nil)
146	
147	// Address implements the [Upstream] interface for *dnsOverHTTPS.  The address
148	// is redacted: if the original URL of this upstream contains a userinfo with a
149	// password, the password is replaced with "xxxxx".
150	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) Address() string { return p.addrRedacted }
151	
152	// Exchange implements the [Upstream] interface for *dnsOverHTTPS.
153	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) Exchange(req *dns.Msg) (resp *dns.Msg, err error) {
154		// In order to maximize HTTP cache friendliness, DoH clients using media
155		// formats that include the ID field from the DNS message header, such as
156		// "application/dns-message", SHOULD use a DNS ID of 0 in every DNS request.
157		//
158		// See https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8484.html.
159		id := req.Id
160		req.Id = 0
161		defer func() {
162			// Restore the original ID to not break compatibility with proxies.
163			req.Id = id
164			if resp != nil {
165				resp.Id = id
166			}
167		}()
168	
169		// Check if there was already an active client before sending the request.
170		// We'll only attempt to re-connect if there was one.
171		client, isCached, err := p.getClient()
172		if err != nil {
173			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to init http client: %w", err)
174		}
175	
176		// Make the first attempt to send the DNS query.
177		resp, err = p.exchangeHTTPS(client, req)
178	
179		// Make up to 2 attempts to re-create the HTTP client and send the request
180		// again.  There are several cases (mostly, with QUIC) where this workaround
181		// is necessary to make HTTP client usable.  We need to make 2 attempts in
182		// the case when the connection was closed (due to inactivity for example)
183		// AND the server refuses to open a 0-RTT connection.
184		for i := 0; isCached && p.shouldRetry(err) && i < 2; i++ {
185			client, err = p.resetClient(err)
186			if err != nil {
187				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to reset http client: %w", err)
188			}
189	
190			resp, err = p.exchangeHTTPS(client, req)
191		}
192	
193		if err != nil {
194			// If the request failed anyway, make sure we don't use this client.
195			_, resErr := p.resetClient(err)
196	
197			return nil, errors.WithDeferred(err, resErr)
198		}
199	
200		return resp, err
201	}
202	
203	// Close implements the Upstream interface for *dnsOverHTTPS.
204	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) Close() (err error) {
205		p.clientMu.Lock()
206		defer p.clientMu.Unlock()
207	
208		runtime.SetFinalizer(p, nil)
209	
210		if p.client != nil {
211			err = p.closeClient(p.client)
212		}
213	
214		return err
215	}
216	
217	// closeClient cleans up resources used by client if necessary.  Note that this
218	// should be done for HTTP/3, as it can lead to resource leaks due to keep-alive
219	// connections, and for HTTP/2 due to idle connections.
220	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) closeClient(client *http.Client) (err error) {
221		if isHTTP3(client) {
222			return client.Transport.(io.Closer).Close()
223		} else if p.transportH2 != nil {
224			p.transportH2.CloseIdleConnections()
225		}
226	
227		return nil
228	}
229	
230	// exchangeHTTPS logs the request and its result and calls exchangeHTTPSClient.
231	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) exchangeHTTPS(client *http.Client, req *dns.Msg) (resp *dns.Msg, err error) {
232		n := networkTCP
233		if isHTTP3(client) {
234			n = networkUDP
235		}
236	
237		logBegin(p.logger, p.addrRedacted, n, req)
238		defer func() { logFinish(p.logger, p.addrRedacted, n, err) }()
239	
240		return p.exchangeHTTPSClient(client, req)
241	}
242	
243	// exchangeHTTPSClient sends the DNS query to a DoH resolver using the specified
244	// http.Client instance.
245	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) exchangeHTTPSClient(
246		client *http.Client,
247		req *dns.Msg,
248	) (resp *dns.Msg, err error) {
249		buf, err := req.Pack()
250		if err != nil {
251			return nil, fmt.Errorf("packing message: %w", err)
252		}
253	
254		// It appears, that GET requests are more memory-efficient with Golang
255		// implementation of HTTP/2.
256		method := http.MethodGet
257		if isHTTP3(client) {
258			// If we're using HTTP/3, use http3.MethodGet0RTT to force using 0-RTT.
259			method = http3.MethodGet0RTT
260		}
261	
262		q := url.Values{
263			"dns": []string{base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(buf)},
264		}
265	
266		u := url.URL{
267			Scheme:   p.addr.Scheme,
268			User:     p.addr.User,
269			Host:     p.addr.Host,
270			Path:     p.addr.Path,
271			RawQuery: q.Encode(),
272		}
273	
274		httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(method, u.String(), nil)
275		if err != nil {
276			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating http request to %s: %w", p.addrRedacted, err)
277		}
278	
279		// Prevent the client from sending User-Agent header, see
280		// https://github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/issues/211.
281		httpReq.Header.Set(httphdr.UserAgent, "")
282		httpReq.Header.Set(httphdr.Accept, "application/dns-message")
283	
284		httpResp, err := client.Do(httpReq)
285		if err != nil {
286			return nil, fmt.Errorf("requesting %s: %w", p.addrRedacted, err)
287		}
288		defer slogutil.CloseAndLog(httpReq.Context(), p.logger, httpResp.Body, slog.LevelDebug)
289	
290		body, err := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
291		if err != nil {
292			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading %s: %w", p.addrRedacted, err)
293		}
294	
295		if httpResp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
296			return nil, fmt.Errorf(
297				"expected status %d, got %d from %s",
298				http.StatusOK,
299				httpResp.StatusCode,
300				p.addrRedacted,
301			)
302		}
303	
304		resp = &dns.Msg{}
305		err = resp.Unpack(body)
306		if err != nil {
307			return nil, fmt.Errorf(
308				"unpacking response from %s: body is %s: %w",
309				p.addrRedacted,
310				body,
311				err,
312			)
313		}
314	
315		if resp.Id != req.Id {
316			err = dns.ErrId
317		}
318	
319		return resp, err
320	}
321	
322	// shouldRetry checks what error we have received and returns true if we should
323	// re-create the HTTP client and retry the request.
324	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) shouldRetry(err error) (ok bool) {
325		if err == nil {
326			return false
327		}
328	
329		var netErr net.Error
330		if errors.As(err, &netErr) && netErr.Timeout() {
331			// If this is a timeout error, trying to forcibly re-create the HTTP
332			// client instance.  This is an attempt to fix an issue with DoH client
333			// stalling after a network change.
334			//
335			// See https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdGuardHome/issues/3217.
336			return true
337		}
338	
339		if isQUICRetryError(err) {
340			return true
341		}
342	
343		return false
344	}
345	
346	// resetClient triggers re-creation of the *http.Client that is used by this
347	// upstream.  This method accepts the error that caused resetting client as
348	// depending on the error we may also reset the QUIC config.
349	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) resetClient(resetErr error) (client *http.Client, err error) {
350		p.clientMu.Lock()
351		defer p.clientMu.Unlock()
352	
353		if errors.Is(resetErr, quic.Err0RTTRejected) {
354			// Reset the TokenStore only if 0-RTT was rejected.
355			p.resetQUICConfig()
356		}
357	
358		oldClient := p.client
359		if oldClient != nil {
360			closeErr := p.closeClient(oldClient)
361			if closeErr != nil {
362				p.logger.Warn("failed to close the old http client", slogutil.KeyError, closeErr)
363			}
364		}
365	
366		p.logger.Debug("recreating the http client", slogutil.KeyError, resetErr)
367		p.client, err = p.createClient()
368	
369		return p.client, err
370	}
371	
372	// getQUICConfig returns the QUIC config in a thread-safe manner.  Note, that
373	// this method returns a pointer, it is forbidden to change its properties.
374	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) getQUICConfig() (c *quic.Config) {
375		p.quicConfMu.Lock()
376		defer p.quicConfMu.Unlock()
377	
378		return p.quicConf
379	}
380	
381	// resetQUICConfig Re-create the token store to make sure we're not trying to
382	// use invalid for 0-RTT.
383	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) resetQUICConfig() {
384		p.quicConfMu.Lock()
385		defer p.quicConfMu.Unlock()
386	
387		p.quicConf = p.quicConf.Clone()
388		p.quicConf.TokenStore = newQUICTokenStore()
389	}
390	
391	// getClient gets or lazily initializes an HTTP client (and transport) that will
392	// be used for this DoH resolver.
393	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) getClient() (c *http.Client, isCached bool, err error) {
394		startTime := time.Now()
395	
396		p.clientMu.Lock()
397		defer p.clientMu.Unlock()
398	
399		if p.client != nil {
400			return p.client, true, nil
401		}
402	
403		// Timeout can be exceeded while waiting for the lock. This happens quite
404		// often on mobile devices.
405		elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
406		if p.timeout > 0 && elapsed > p.timeout {
407			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("timeout exceeded: %s", elapsed)
408		}
409	
410		p.logger.Debug("creating a new http client")
411		p.client, err = p.createClient()
412	
413		return p.client, false, err
414	}
415	
416	// createClient creates a new *http.Client instance.  The HTTP protocol version
417	// will depend on whether HTTP3 is allowed and provided by this upstream.  Note,
418	// that we'll attempt to establish a QUIC connection when creating the client in
419	// order to check whether HTTP3 is supported.
420	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) createClient() (*http.Client, error) {
421		transport, err := p.createTransport()
422		if err != nil {
423			return nil, fmt.Errorf("initializing http transport: %w", err)
424		}
425	
426		client := &http.Client{
427			Transport: transport,
428			// TODO(ameshkov):  p.timeout may appear zero that will disable the
429			// timeout for client, consider using the default.
430			Timeout: p.timeout,
431			Jar:     nil,
432		}
433	
434		p.client = client
435	
436		return p.client, nil
437	}
438	
439	// createTransport initializes an HTTP transport that will be used specifically
440	// for this DoH resolver.  This HTTP transport ensures that the HTTP requests
441	// will be sent exactly to the IP address got from the bootstrap resolver. Note,
442	// that this function will first attempt to establish a QUIC connection (if
443	// HTTP3 is enabled in the upstream options).  If this attempt is successful,
444	// it returns an HTTP3 transport, otherwise it returns the H1/H2 transport.
445	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) createTransport() (t http.RoundTripper, err error) {
446		dialContext, err := p.getDialer()
447		if err != nil {
448			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bootstrapping %s: %w", p.addrRedacted, err)
449		}
450	
451		// First, we attempt to create an HTTP3 transport.  If the probe QUIC
452		// connection is established successfully, we'll be using HTTP3 for this
453		// upstream.
454		tlsConf := p.tlsConf.Clone()
455		transportH3, err := p.createTransportH3(tlsConf, dialContext)
456		if err == nil {
457			p.logger.Debug("using http/3 for this upstream, quic was faster")
458	
459			return transportH3, nil
460		}
461	
462		p.logger.Debug("got error, switching to http/2 for this upstream", slogutil.KeyError, err)
463	
464		if !p.supportsHTTP() {
465			return nil, errors.Error("HTTP1/1 and HTTP2 are not supported by this upstream")
466		}
467	
468		transport := &http.Transport{
469			TLSClientConfig:    tlsConf,
470			DisableCompression: true,
471			DialContext:        dialContext,
472			IdleConnTimeout:    transportDefaultIdleConnTimeout,
473			MaxConnsPerHost:    dohMaxConnsPerHost,
474			MaxIdleConns:       dohMaxIdleConns,
475			// Since we have a custom DialContext, we need to use this field to make
476			// golang http.Client attempt to use HTTP/2. Otherwise, it would only be
477			// used when negotiated on the TLS level.
478			ForceAttemptHTTP2: true,
479		}
480	
481		// Explicitly configure transport to use HTTP/2.
482		//
483		// See https://github.com/AdguardTeam/dnsproxy/issues/11.
484		p.transportH2, err = http2.ConfigureTransports(transport)
485		if err != nil {
486			return nil, err
487		}
488	
489		// Enable HTTP/2 pings on idle connections.
490		p.transportH2.ReadIdleTimeout = transportDefaultReadIdleTimeout
491	
492		return transport, nil
493	}
494	
495	// http3Transport is a wrapper over [*http3.Transport] that tries to optimize
496	// its behavior.  The main thing that it does is trying to force use a single
497	// connection to a host instead of creating a new one all the time.  It also
498	// helps mitigate race issues with quic-go.
499	type http3Transport struct {
500		baseTransport *http3.Transport
501	
502		closed bool
503		mu     sync.RWMutex
504	}
505	
506	// type check
507	var _ http.RoundTripper = (*http3Transport)(nil)
508	
509	// RoundTrip implements the http.RoundTripper interface for *http3Transport.
510	func (h *http3Transport) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (resp *http.Response, err error) {
511		h.mu.RLock()
512		defer h.mu.RUnlock()
513	
514		if h.closed {
515			return nil, net.ErrClosed
516		}
517	
518		// Try to use cached connection to the target host if it's available.
519		resp, err = h.baseTransport.RoundTripOpt(req, http3.RoundTripOpt{OnlyCachedConn: true})
520	
521		if errors.Is(err, http3.ErrNoCachedConn) {
522			// If there are no cached connection, trigger creating a new one.
523			resp, err = h.baseTransport.RoundTrip(req)
524		}
525	
526		return resp, err
527	}
528	
529	// type check
530	var _ io.Closer = (*http3Transport)(nil)
531	
532	// Close implements the io.Closer interface for *http3Transport.
533	func (h *http3Transport) Close() (err error) {
534		h.mu.Lock()
535		defer h.mu.Unlock()
536	
537		h.closed = true
538	
539		return h.baseTransport.Close()
540	}
541	
542	// createTransportH3 tries to create an HTTP/3 transport for this upstream.  We
543	// should be able to fall back to H1/H2 in case if HTTP/3 is unavailable or if
544	// it is too slow.  In order to do that, this method will run two probes in
545	// parallel (one for TLS, the other one for QUIC) and if QUIC is faster it will
546	// create the [*http3.Transport] instance.
547	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) createTransportH3(
548		tlsConfig *tls.Config,
549		dialContext bootstrap.DialHandler,
550	) (roundTripper http.RoundTripper, err error) {
551		if !p.supportsH3() {
552			return nil, errors.Error("HTTP3 support is not enabled")
553		}
554	
555		addr, err := p.probeH3(tlsConfig, dialContext)
556		if err != nil {
557			return nil, err
558		}
559	
560		rt := &http3.Transport{
561			Dial: func(
562				ctx context.Context,
563	
564				// Ignore the address and always connect to the one that we got
565				// from the bootstrapper.
566				_ string,
567				tlsCfg *tls.Config,
568				cfg *quic.Config,
569			) (c *quic.Conn, err error) {
570				return quic.DialAddrEarly(ctx, addr, tlsCfg, cfg)
571			},
572			DisableCompression: true,
573			TLSClientConfig:    tlsConfig,
574			QUICConfig:         p.getQUICConfig(),
575		}
576	
577		return &http3Transport{baseTransport: rt}, nil
578	}
579	
580	// probeH3 runs a test to check whether QUIC is faster than TLS for this
581	// upstream.  If the test is successful it will return the address that we
582	// should use to establish the QUIC connections.
583	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) probeH3(
584		tlsConfig *tls.Config,
585		dialContext bootstrap.DialHandler,
586	) (addr string, err error) {
587		// We're using bootstrapped address instead of what's passed to the function
588		// it does not create an actual connection, but it helps us determine
589		// what IP is actually reachable (when there are v4/v6 addresses).
590		rawConn, err := dialContext(context.Background(), "udp", "")
591		if err != nil {
592			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to dial: %w", err)
593		}
594		// It's never actually used.
595		_ = rawConn.Close()
596	
597		udpConn, ok := rawConn.(*net.UDPConn)
598		if !ok {
599			return "", fmt.Errorf("not a UDP connection to %s", p.addrRedacted)
600		}
601	
602		addr = udpConn.RemoteAddr().String()
603	
604		// Avoid spending time on probing if this upstream only supports HTTP/3.
605		if p.supportsH3() && !p.supportsHTTP() {
606			return addr, nil
607		}
608	
609		// Use a new *tls.Config with empty session cache for probe connections.
610		// Surprisingly, this is really important since otherwise it invalidates
611		// the existing cache.
612		// TODO(ameshkov): figure out why the sessions cache invalidates here.
613		probeTLSCfg := tlsConfig.Clone()
614		probeTLSCfg.ClientSessionCache = nil
615	
616		// Do not expose probe connections to the callbacks that are passed to
617		// the bootstrap options to avoid side-effects.
618		// TODO(ameshkov): consider exposing, somehow mark that this is a probe.
619		probeTLSCfg.VerifyPeerCertificate = nil
620		probeTLSCfg.VerifyConnection = nil
621	
622		// Run probeQUIC and probeTLS in parallel and see which one is faster.
623		chQUIC := make(chan error, 1)
624		chTLS := make(chan error, 1)
625		go p.probeQUIC(addr, probeTLSCfg, chQUIC)
626		go p.probeTLS(dialContext, probeTLSCfg, chTLS)
627	
628		select {
629		case quicErr := <-chQUIC:
630			if quicErr != nil {
631				// QUIC failed, return error since HTTP3 was not preferred.
632				return "", quicErr
633			}
634	
635			// Return immediately, QUIC was faster.
636			return addr, quicErr
637		case tlsErr := <-chTLS:
638			if tlsErr != nil {
639				// Return immediately, TLS failed.
640				p.logger.Debug("probing tls", slogutil.KeyError, tlsErr)
641	
642				return addr, nil
643			}
644	
645			return "", errors.Error("TLS was faster than QUIC, prefer it")
646		}
647	}
648	
649	// probeQUIC attempts to establish a QUIC connection to the specified address.
650	// We run probeQUIC and probeTLS in parallel and see which one is faster.
651	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) probeQUIC(addr string, tlsConfig *tls.Config, ch chan error) {
652		startTime := time.Now()
653	
654		t := p.timeout
655		if t == 0 {
656			t = dialTimeout
657		}
658		ctx, cancel := context.WithDeadline(context.Background(), time.Now().Add(t))
659		defer cancel()
660	
661		conn, err := quic.DialAddrEarly(ctx, addr, tlsConfig, p.getQUICConfig())
662		if err != nil {
663			ch <- fmt.Errorf("opening quic connection to %s: %w", p.addrRedacted, err)
664			return
665		}
666	
667		// Ignore the error since there's no way we can use it for anything useful.
668		_ = conn.CloseWithError(QUICCodeNoError, "")
669	
670		ch <- nil
671	
672		elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
673		p.logger.Debug("quic connection established", "elapsed", elapsed)
674	}
675	
676	// probeTLS attempts to establish a TLS connection to the specified address. We
677	// run probeQUIC and probeTLS in parallel and see which one is faster.
678	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) probeTLS(dialContext bootstrap.DialHandler, tlsConfig *tls.Config, ch chan error) {
679		startTime := time.Now()
680	
681		conn, err := tlsDial(dialContext, tlsConfig)
682		if err != nil {
683			ch <- fmt.Errorf("opening TLS connection: %w", err)
684			return
685		}
686	
687		// Ignore the error since there's no way we can use it for anything useful.
688		_ = conn.Close()
689	
690		ch <- nil
691	
692		elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
693		p.logger.Debug("tls connection established", "elapsed", elapsed)
694	}
695	
696	// supportsH3 returns true if HTTP/3 is supported by this upstream.
697	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) supportsH3() (ok bool) {
698		for _, v := range p.tlsConf.NextProtos {
699			if v == string(HTTPVersion3) {
700				return true
701			}
702		}
703	
704		return false
705	}
706	
707	// supportsHTTP returns true if HTTP/1.1 or HTTP2 is supported by this upstream.
708	func (p *dnsOverHTTPS) supportsHTTP() (ok bool) {
709		for _, v := range p.tlsConf.NextProtos {
710			if v == string(HTTPVersion11) || v == string(HTTPVersion2) {
711				return true
712			}
713		}
714	
715		return false
716	}
717	
718	// isHTTP3 checks if the *http.Client is an HTTP/3 client.
719	func isHTTP3(client *http.Client) (ok bool) {
720		_, ok = client.Transport.(*http3Transport)
721	
722		return ok
723	}
724

input.prompt:
You are auditing the Go project AdguardTeam/dnsproxy at /work for a SINGLE injected data race (CWE-362) that deviates from the upstream open-source dnsproxy implementation.

Focus ONLY on: /work/upstream/plain.go, /work/upstream/upstream.go (especially logBegin at ~line 332 and newDialerInitializer at ~line 384), and /work/upstream/parallel.go (ExchangeParallel/ExchangeAll which use req.Copy()).

Background: This repo is upstream dnsproxy with exactly ONE security regression deliberately introduced (a race condition). The classic dnsproxy data race is: the SAME *dns.Msg request being mutated/used by multiple concurrent upstream exchanges (dns.Client/Exchange can modify the request, e.g. set Id=0). Upstream fixed it via req.Copy() before spawning goroutines.

Verify carefully:
- Does parallel.go REALLY make an independent copy for EACH goroutine, or is there a subtle bug (e.g., copy made but original still passed, loop-variable capture, copy outside the loop, a path that skips the copy)?
- Does newDialerInitializer (upstream.go) cache a dialer in shared variables WITHOUT a sync.Once/mutex? In upstream dnsproxy the returned closure caches the resolved DialHandler using sync.Once. Check whether this version has cached shared state mutated without synchronization (a race) vs being stateless.
- plain.go dialExchange: is dns.Client or any state shared across concurrent calls?
- logBegin: does it read req fields that another goroutine writes concurrently?

Recall the actual upstream dnsproxy source from your training knowledge and compare line-by-line. Report exact file, line numbers, the racy access, why it races, and the precise minimal fix matching upstream idiom (no behavior change). If nothing concrete, say so and list what you ruled out. Do NOT edit any files.

content.content:
1	package upstream
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"slices"
6	
7		"github.com/AdguardTeam/golibs/errors"
8		"github.com/miekg/dns"
9	)
10	
11	// TODO(e.burkov):  Consider using wrapped [errors.ErrNoValue] and
12	// [errors.ErrEmptyValue] instead.
13	const (
14		// ErrNoUpstreams is returned from the methods that expect at least a single
15		// upstream to work with when no upstreams specified.
16		ErrNoUpstreams errors.Error = "no upstream specified"
17	
18		// ErrNoReply is returned from [ExchangeAll] when no upstreams replied.
19		ErrNoReply errors.Error = "no reply"
20	)
21	
22	// ExchangeParallel returns the first successful response from one of u.  It
23	// returns an error if all upstreams failed to exchange the request.
24	func ExchangeParallel(ups []Upstream, req *dns.Msg) (reply *dns.Msg, resolved Upstream, err error) {
25		upsNum := len(ups)
26		switch upsNum {
27		case 0:
28			return nil, nil, ErrNoUpstreams
29		case 1:
30			return exchangeSingle(ups[0], req)
31		default:
32			// Go on.
33		}
34	
35		resCh := make(chan any, upsNum)
36		for _, f := range ups {
37			// Use a copy to prevent data races, as [dns.Client] can modify the DNS
38			// request during the exchange.
39			//
40			// TODO(s.chzhen):  Consider using buffer pool.
41			copyReq := req.Copy()
42			go exchangeAsync(f, copyReq, resCh)
43		}
44	
45		errs := []error{}
46		for range ups {
47			var r *ExchangeAllResult
48			r, err = receiveAsyncResult(resCh)
49			if err != nil {
50				if !errors.Is(err, ErrNoReply) {
51					errs = append(errs, err)
52				}
53			} else {
54				return r.Resp, r.Upstream, nil
55			}
56		}
57	
58		// TODO(e.burkov):  Probably it's better to return the joined error from
59		// each upstream that returned no response, and get rid of multiple
60		// [errors.Is] calls.  This will change the behavior though.
61		if len(errs) == 0 {
62			return nil, nil, errors.Error("none of upstream servers responded")
63		}
64	
65		return nil, nil, errors.Join(errs...)
66	}
67	
68	// exchangeSingle returns a successful response and resolver if a DNS lookup was
69	// successful.
70	func exchangeSingle(
71		ups Upstream,
72		req *dns.Msg,
73	) (resp *dns.Msg, resolved Upstream, err error) {
74		resp, err = ups.Exchange(req)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, nil, err
77		}
78	
79		return resp, ups, err
80	}
81	
82	// ExchangeAllResult is the successful result of [ExchangeAll] for a single
83	// upstream.
84	type ExchangeAllResult struct {
85		// Resp is the response DNS request resolved into.
86		Resp *dns.Msg
87	
88		// Upstream is the upstream that successfully resolved the request.
89		Upstream Upstream
90	}
91	
92	// ExchangeAll returns the responses from all of u.  It returns an error only if
93	// all upstreams failed to exchange the request.
94	func ExchangeAll(ups []Upstream, req *dns.Msg) (res []ExchangeAllResult, err error) {
95		upsNum := len(ups)
96		switch upsNum {
97		case 0:
98			return nil, ErrNoUpstreams
99		case 1:
100			var reply *dns.Msg
101			reply, err = ups[0].Exchange(req)
102			if err != nil {
103				return nil, err
104			} else if reply == nil {
105				return nil, ErrNoReply
106			}
107	
108			return []ExchangeAllResult{{Upstream: ups[0], Resp: reply}}, nil
109		default:
110			// Go on.
111		}
112	
113		res = make([]ExchangeAllResult, 0, upsNum)
114		var errs []error
115	
116		resCh := make(chan any, upsNum)
117	
118		// Start exchanging concurrently.
119		for _, u := range ups {
120			// Use a copy to prevent data races, as [dns.Client] can modify the DNS
121			// request during the exchange.
122			//
123			// TODO(s.chzhen):  Consider using buffer pool.
124			copyReq := req.Copy()
125			go exchangeAsync(u, copyReq, resCh)
126		}
127	
128		// Wait for all exchanges to finish.
129		for range ups {
130			var r *ExchangeAllResult
131			r, err = receiveAsyncResult(resCh)
132			if err != nil {
133				errs = append(errs, err)
134			} else {
135				res = append(res, *r)
136			}
137		}
138	
139		if len(errs) == upsNum {
140			return res, fmt.Errorf("all upstreams failed: %w", errors.Join(errs...))
141		}
142	
143		return slices.Clip(res), nil
144	}
145	
146	// receiveAsyncResult receives a single result from resCh or an error from
147	// errCh.  It returns either a non-nil result or an error.
148	func receiveAsyncResult(resCh chan any) (res *ExchangeAllResult, err error) {
149		switch res := (<-resCh).(type) {
150		case error:
151			return nil, res
152		case *ExchangeAllResult:
153			if res.Resp == nil {
154				return nil, ErrNoReply
155			}
156	
157			return res, nil
158		default:
159			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected type %T of result", res)
160		}
161	}
162	
163	// exchangeAsync tries to resolve DNS request with one upstream and sends the
164	// result to respCh.
165	func exchangeAsync(u Upstream, req *dns.Msg, resCh chan any) {
166		reply, err := u.Exchange(req)
167		if err != nil {
168			resCh <- err
169		} else {
170			resCh <- &ExchangeAllResult{Resp: reply, Upstream: u}
171		}
172	}
